chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No.S-370 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 17-10-2025 Mr. Tariq Rehman Shaikh, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Assistant A.G. Mr. Sardar Ali Solangi, D.P.G for the State. ************** Respondents No.2 and 3 have filed their respective statements/comments, which are taken on record. Mr. Amir Muhammad Dahani, Advocate, files Vakalatnama on behalf of respondents No.6 and 7, which is also taken on record. In his statement, respondent No.2 has denied the allegations levelled against him and submits that he has never harassed the petitioner, nor does he intend to do so. He further assures that he shall act strictly in accordance with law. Learned Assistant Advocate General also submits that the official respondents are legally bound to act in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner as and when required. From a perusal of the petition, it appears that the matter primarily pertains to a civil dispute between the parties, for which the petitioner has approached this Court. However, this Court cannot adjudicate disputed questions of fact or enter into civil controversies between private parties in its constitutional jurisdiction. The petitioner and/or the private respondents, if aggrieved by any action or omission arising out of a civil dispute, may seek appropriate remedy before the competent forum having jurisdiction, in accordance with law. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner.

The petitioner and/or the private respondents, if aggrieved by any action or omission arising out of a civil dispute, may seek appropriate remedy before the competent forum having jurisdiction, in accordance with law. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh for information and necessary compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
